content:
We’ve Been Solving the Wrong Problem
For the past few years, most AI research has focused on improving the model itself.
Bigger parameter counts.
Larger context windows.
More training data.
Better reasoning benchmarks.
These advances are important.
But production AI systems rarely fail because the model cannot answer a question.
They fail because the surrounding infrastructure cannot consistently provide the right information, maintain long-term context, enforce business rules, coordinate tools, or recover from errors.
In other words, the intelligence exists.
The system doesn’t.
